stimulus: When composing Symphony No. 9 (2018), Chinese American composer Tan Dun drew on traditional folk melodies he had learned in childhood. The acclaimed work is Tan's ninth symphony, but his composing ______blank recognized before. At the age of nineteen, he submitted a piece to a national music competition—and won.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The convention being tested is subject-verb agreement. The singular verb "has been" agrees in number with the singular subject "composing."

Choice A is incorrect because the plural verb "are" doesn't agree in number with the singular subject "composing." Choice C is incorrect because the plural verb "have been" doesn't agree in number with the singular subject "composing." Choice D is incorrect because the plural verb "were" doesn't agree in number with the singular subject "composing."
